---
title: "Untitled"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
date: "2026-03-29"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r include=FALSE}
#install.packages("cansim")
#install.packages("dplyr")
#install.packages("tidyr")
#install.packages("tidyverse")
#install.packages("stats")
#install.packages("readr")
#install.packages("stringr")
#install.packages("ggplot2")
#install.packages("effects")
#install.packages("lme4")
#install.packages("MASS")
library(cansim)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(stats)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(effects)
#library(lme4)
library(dplyr)
#select <- dplyr::select
```

```{r}
df_age <- get_cansim("35-10-006-01")
df_geo <- get_cansim("35-10-0156-01")
#df_total <-get_cansim("35-10-0068-01")
df_indID <- get_cansim("35-10-0125-01")
#df_urb <-get_cansim("35-10-0127-01")
df_pop  <- read_csv("df_pop1.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

```

```{r}
# Standardize key columns across tables
df_geo <- df_geo %>% 
  mutate(year = REF_DATE,
         GEO = GEO,
         gender = Gender,
         ind_id = `Indigenous identity`,
         stat = Homicides,
         value = VALUE) %>%
  select(year, GEO, gender, ind_id, stat, value)

#filter for all rows where ind_id equals Indigenous identity
df_geo <- df_geo %>%
  filter(ind_id =="Indigenous identity")


df_pop21 <- df_pop %>%
  filter(str_detect(`Statistics (5)`,"2021")) %>%
  mutate(year = "2021",
         GEO = GEO,
         gender = `Gender (3a)`,
         age = `Age (8G)`,
         ind_id = `Indigenous identity (9)`,
         stat = `Statistics (5)`,
         VALUE = VALUE) %>%
  select(year, GEO, gender, age, ind_id, stat, VALUE) %>%
  filter(!is.na(VALUE))

df_pop16 <- df_pop %>%
  filter(str_detect(`Statistics (5)`,"2016")) %>%
  mutate(year = "2016",
         GEO = GEO,
         gender = `Gender (3a)`,
         age = `Age (8G)`,
         ind_id = `Indigenous identity (9)`,
         stat = `Statistics (5)`,
         VALUE = VALUE) %>%
  select(year, GEO, gender, age, ind_id, stat, VALUE) %>%
  filter(!is.na(VALUE))

df_pop <- rbind(df_pop16,df_pop21)
df_pop$gender <- df_pop$gender %>%
  replace(df_pop$gender=="Women+", "Female") %>%
  replace(df_pop$gender =="Men+", "Male") %>%
  replace(df_pop$gender == "Total - Gender", "All genders")

df_pop$age <- df_pop$age %>%
  replace(df_pop$age =="Total - Age", "Total all ages")

#filter out total by Indigenous identity (includes non-Indigenous identities)
df_pop <- df_pop %>%
  filter(df_pop$ind_id != "Total - Indigenous identity")

##Spread the values column along the statistics
df_pop$stat <- df_pop$stat %>%
  replace(df_pop$stat == "% distribution (2016)", "rate_ind") %>%
  replace(df_pop$stat == "% distribution (2021)", "rate_ind") %>%
  replace(df_pop$stat == "2016 Counts", "pop") %>%
  replace(df_pop$stat == "2021 Counts", "pop")
df_pop <- df_pop %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = stat, values_from = VALUE)
  
```

```{r}
#select rows from different attribute tables using str_detect 
#creating victims tables 
victims_indID <- df_indID %>%
  filter(str_detect(Statistics, "Number of homicide victims"),
         str_detect(`Indigenous Identity`, "First Nations|Métis|Inuk|Indigenous person"),
         `Indigenous Identity`!= "Non-Indigenous identity",
         `Indigenous Identity`!= "Total, by Indigenous identity") %>%
  mutate(ind_type = case_when(
    `Indigenous Identity`=="Indigenous person, Indigenous group unknown"~"Indigenous identity", TRUE ~ `Indigenous Identity`))%>%
  select(year = REF_DATE, GEO = GEO, gender = Gender, ind_type, victims = VALUE)


victims_geo <- df_geo %>%
  filter(str_detect(stat, "Number of homicide victims"),
         ind_id=="Indigenous identity") %>%
  select(year , GEO, gender, ind_id, victims = value)

victims_age <- df_age %>%
  filter(str_detect(`Victims and accused`, "Number of homicide victims"),
         `Indigenous identity`== "Indigenous identity",) %>%
  select(year = REF_DATE, GEO = GEO, gender = Gender, ind_type = `Indigenous identity`, age =`Age group`, victims = VALUE)
victims_age<- victims_age %>%
  filter(!is.na(victims),
  gender!="Gender unknown", 
  age != "Age unknown")

#victims_urb <- df_urb %>%
 # filter(str_detect(Statistics, "Number of homicide victims"),
  #       str_detect(`Indigenous identity`, "First Nations|Métis|Inuk|Indigenous person"),
   #      `Indigenous identity`!= "Non-Indigenous identity",
    #     `Indigenous identity`!= "Total, by Indigenous identity") %>%
#  mutate(ind_type = case_when(
 #   str_detect(`Indigenous identity`, "First Nations") ~ "First Nations",
  #  str_detect(`Indigenous identity`, "Métis") ~ "Métis",
   # str_detect(`Indigenous identity`, "Inuk|Inuit") ~ "Inuit",
#    TRUE ~ "Indigenous (total)")) %>%
#  select(year = REF_DATE, GEO = GEO, ind_type, location = `Location of homicide` , victims = VALUE)

##URB/Rural data cannot compute rates for regression
```

```{r}
##harmonize ages across tables
victims_age<- victims_age %>%
  mutate(
    age_harmonized = case_when(
      age == "Total all ages"                  ~ "Total all ages",
      age == "0 to 11 years"              ~ "0 to 14 years",     # Note: includes some 12-14
      age %in% c("12 to 17 years", "18 to 24 years") ~ "15 to 24 years",
      age == "25 to 29 years"             ~ "25 to 34 years",
      age == "30 to 39 years"                  ~ "25 to 34 years",    # or "35 to 44 years" — choose one consistently
      age == "40 to 49 years"                  ~ "35 to 44 years",
      age == "50 to 59 years"                  ~ "45 to 54 years",     # or "55 to 64 years"
      age == "60 years and over"               ~ "55 to 64 years",     # This is imperfect (misses some 65+ weighting)
      age == "Age unknown"                     ~ NA_character_
    )
  )


```

```{r}
##bind all the victim counts rows
total_counts <- bind_rows(victims_geo, victims_age, victims_indID) %>%
  distinct(year, GEO, gender, age_harmonized, ind_type, victims, .keep_all = TRUE)

total_counts <- total_counts %>%
  filter(gender != "Gender unknown",
         gender != "Unidentifiable human remains")

total_counts <- total_counts %>%
  mutate(
    ind_type = ind_type %>%
      replace_values( NA ~ "Indigenous identity")) %>%
  select(year, GEO, gender, ind_type, age_harmonized, victims)
 
  
```

```{r}
##join requires equating indigenous identity across tables -> pop table
df_pop2<- df_pop %>%
  filter(ind_id != "Single Indigenous responses") %>%
  mutate(ind_id = ind_id %>%
            replace_values("Indigenous identity" ~ "Indigenous identity (Total)"))%>%
  select(year, GEO, gender,age, ind_id, pop)

df_pop3 <- df_pop2 %>%
  mutate(ind_id = ind_id%>%
    replace_values(
    "Multiple Indigenous responses" ~ "Indigenous identity", 
    "Indigenous responses not included elsewhere"~"Indigenous identity")) %>%
  group_by(year, gender, age, GEO, ind_id)%>%
  summarise(pop = sum(pop),n= n())

df_pop3<- df_pop3 %>%
  filter(n==2)%>%
  ungroup()%>%
  select(year, GEO, gender,age, ind_id, pop)

df_pop <-rbind(df_pop2, df_pop3)%>%
  filter(ind_id !="Multiple Indigenous responses",
         ind_id !="Indigenous responses not included elsewhere")

```




```{r}
##try without age join remove from total counts
#total_counts1 <- total_counts[-c(5)]

df_master <- total_counts %>%
  left_join(df_pop, by = c("year" = "year", "GEO" = "GEO", "gender" = "gender", 
                             "ind_type" = "ind_id")) #%>%

##remove NA in victim columns and also move col
df_master <- df_master %>% drop_na(victims,pop)

# Compute rate per 100000
df_master<- df_master %>%
  mutate(rate_ind =  (victims / pop) * 100000)%>%
  select(year,GEO,gender,ind_type,victims,rate_ind)

#_____________________________________________________________________________      
                                 
#remove age col from total counts
total_counts<- filter(total_counts, is.na(age_harmonized))
total_counts<- total_counts[-c(5)]

```

```{r}
mean(total_counts$victims)
var(total_counts$victims)
#mean(df_canada$victims)
#var(df_canada$victims)
hist(total_counts$victims)
#hist(df_canada$victims)
```

```{r}
#subset by prov
df_canada<- filter(total_counts, GEO =="Canada")
df_canada$"Log_victims" <- log(df_canada$victims)
df_canada <- filter(df_canada, gender != "All genders")
df_canada <- filter(df_canada, Log_victims != "-Inf")
```

```{r}

#model_canada <- glm(as.integer(Log_victims) ~ gender + ind_type, data = df_canada, family = "poisson")
#summary(model_canada)

#Non-linear model works better
#model_canada_log <- lm(Log_victims ~ year + gender + ind_type, data = df_canada)
#summary(model_canada_log)
```
```{r}
##summary(pearson_residuals)

#pearson_residuals_canada_log <- residuals(model_canada_log, type = "pearson")
#summary(pearson_residuals_canada_log)
```

```{r}
#total_counts$"Log_victims" <- log(total_counts$victims)
#total_counts <- filter(total_counts, Log_victims != "-Inf")
```


```{r}
#model_totals <-glm(victims  ~ year + GEO + gender + ind_type, data = total_counts, family = poisson)
#summary(model_totals)

#model_totals_log <- lm(Log_victims ~ year + GEO + gender + ind_type, data = total_counts)
#summary(model_totals_log)
```
```{r}
#pearson_residuals_totals <- residuals(model_totals, type = "pearson")
#summary(pearson_residuals_totals)

#pearson_residuals_totals_log <- residuals(model_totals_log, type = "pearson")
#summary(pearson_residuals_totals_log)
```

```{r}
##exp(confint(model_canada))
```

```{r}
model_canada2 <- glm(Log_victims ~ gender + ind_type, data = df_canada, family = gaussian())
summary(model_canada2)
```

```{r}
pearson_residuals <- residuals(model_canada2, type = "pearson")
summary(pearson_residuals)
```

```{r}
exp(coef(model_canada2))
exp(confint(model_canada2))
```

```{r}
model_canada_lm <- lm(Log_victims ~ gender + ind_type, data = df_canada)
summary(model_canada_lm)
```

```{r}
pearson_residuals <- residuals(model_canada_lm, type = "pearson")
summary(pearson_residuals)
```

```{r}
exp(coef(model_canada_lm))
exp(confint(model_canada_lm))
```

```{r}
summary(model_canada2)

# Check residuals and fitted values
plot(model_canada2)         

# Compare AIC with Poisson and lm model 
AIC(model_canada2)
AIC(model_canada)
AIC(model_canada_lm)
```

```{r}
model_canada3 <- glm(Log_victims ~ year + gender * ind_type, data = df_canada, family = gaussian())
summary(model_canada3)
```

```{r}
pearson_residuals <- residuals(model_canada3, type = "pearson")
summary(pearson_residuals)
```

```{r}
exp(coef(model_canada3))
exp(confint(model_canada3))
```

```{r}

```
